ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Analyzes and develops policies to promote agricultural growth and development, ensuring food security and sustainable agriculture practices.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agricultural Policy Analysts work with government agencies, NGOs, and private organizations to design and implement policies that support agricultural development, improve crop yields, and enhance farmer livelihoods. They analyze data, conduct research, and engage with stakeholders to inform policy decisions.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and policy can pursue a Bachelor's degree in Agriculture, Economics, or a related field.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a Master's degree in Agriculture, Economics, or a related field to transition into this role.

future_builder.description: Agricultural professionals with experience in policy analysis, research, or extension services can advance to this role by developing advanced skills in data analysis, policy evaluation, and stakeholder engagement.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience in agricultural policy, research, or extension services
advanced_skills_to_learn: Data Analysis using R or Python

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into this role by developing relevant skills in agriculture, economics, and policy analysis.

roadmap.title: Developing Advanced Skills
topics: Data Analysis using R or Python
